items.title: Consumer Tech News (Feb 3-Feb 7): Meta Plans Not To Comply With The EU AI Code Of Practice, Apple Raises AppleCare+ Service' Subscription Cost & More
items.author: Lekha Gupta
items.description: Meta plans to not comply with the EU AI Code of Practice, Apple raises AppleCare+ subscription cost, and other tech news including updates on Alphabet, OpenAI, Spotify, Take-Two, Palantir, and more.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Apple has increased the monthly subscription cost for its AppleCare+ service across all iPhone models in the U.S.
AMD CEO Lisa Su has addressed DeepSeek's AI advancements and their potential impact on the semiconductor market.
Amazon is gearing up to introduce a major AI upgrade to its Alexa voice assistant.

A federal appeals court in San Francisco showed skepticism toward Alphabet's efforts to overturn a jury verdict in favor of Fortine-maker Epic Games. Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai hinted at potential future monetization strategies for Gemini AI.

A federal appeals court in San Francisco showed skepticism toward Alphabet's efforts to overturn a jury verdict in favor of Fortine-maker Epic Games. Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai hinted at potential future monetization strategies for Gemini AI.
Meta decided not to comply with the European Union's AI Code of Practice.
Microsoft has quietly removed a workaround that allowed users to install Windows 11 on unsupported PCs. Microsoft has recruited top talent from Google DeepMind.
Nvidia's GeForce RTX 5090 and 5080 graphics cards have sold out almost immediately after their release.
Palantir reported fourth-quarter revenue and adjusted earnings that beat analyst estimates.
Spotify reported fourth-quarter earnings that missed the analyst consensus estimate.
Take-Two reported third-quarter revenue and adjusted earnings that beat analyst estimates.

items.title: Should You Forget Amazon? Why You Might Want to Buy This Unstoppable Growth Stock Instead
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The article suggests that Shopify, an e-commerce platform provider, may be a better investment option than Amazon. Shopify offers businesses a personalized and authentic experience, which is increasingly preferred by consumers over price and convenience. The article highlights Shopify's strong growth and the potential for further expansion in the e-commerce market.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article acknowledges Amazon's dominance in the e-commerce market, but suggests that Shopify may be a better investment option due to its ability to provide a more personalized and authentic experience for businesses and consumers.

The article highlights Shopify's strong growth, its ability to help businesses build and manage their own e-commerce presence, and the increasing demand for personalized and authentic experiences in the e-commerce industry. The article suggests that Shopify is well-positioned to capitalize on these trends and continue growing.

items.title: What's Going on With AMD Stock?
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: AMD's stock has been slumping despite strong revenue and earnings growth, due to concerns about its AI accelerator outlook, weakness in gaming and embedded segments, and potential slowdown in CPU market share gains.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights several issues that are weighing on AMD's stock, including a disappointing outlook for its AI accelerator business, weakness in the gaming and embedded segments, and the potential for CPU market share gains to slow. These factors have led to a nearly 50% plunge in AMD's stock price over the past year, despite the company's strong financial performance.

The article notes that Nvidia, the market leader in data center revenue, is outselling AMD by more than 20-fold, suggesting that AMD has a significant opportunity to gain market share in the AI accelerator market.

The article mentions that Microsoft, along with other tech giants like Meta Platforms, Alphabet, and Amazon, have announced audacious capital spending plans to boost their AI compute capacity, which could benefit AMD's AI accelerator business.

The article mentions that Meta Platforms, along with other tech giants, have announced audacious capital spending plans to boost their AI compute capacity, which could benefit AMD's AI accelerator business.

The article mentions that Alphabet, along with other tech giants, have announced audacious capital spending plans to boost their AI compute capacity, which could benefit AMD's AI accelerator business.

The article mentions that Amazon, along with other tech giants, have announced audacious capital spending plans to boost their AI compute capacity, which could benefit AMD's AI accelerator business.
The article suggests that Intel has been getting more aggressive about stabilizing its market share in the PC and data center CPU markets, which could slow down AMD's growth in these areas.

items.title: Best Stock to Buy Right Now: Amazon vs. Carvana
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Carvana and Amazon are two e-commerce giants, but their stock performance has been vastly different over the past two years. Carvana's stock bounced back after a steep decline, while Amazon's growth has slowed. The article analyzes the future prospects of both companies and suggests that Amazon may be the better buy due to its robust growth potential and reasonable valuation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Carvana's stock has seen wild swings, with a steep decline followed by a remarkable comeback. While its growth potential is promising, elevated interest rates could limit its upside in the near term.

Amazon's business is expected to warm up again as the macro environment improves, and its e-commerce and cloud growth engines are still strong. The article suggests that Amazon may be the better buy due to its robust growth potential and reasonable valuation.

items.title: Arm Holdings' Shares Slip Despite Record Revenue and Strong AI Demand. Is This a Golden Buying Opportunity?
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Arm Holdings reported record revenue and strong demand for its AI-focused Armv9 technology, but its stock price slipped. The article analyzes whether this dip presents a buying opportunity for investors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Arm's strong financial performance, with record revenue and growth in its Armv9 technology and data center business. While the stock price dipped, the article suggests that Arm's long-term prospects remain positive.
The article mentions Amazon's Graviton CPU as a solid contributor to Arm's data center business, indicating a positive relationship between the two companies.
The article discusses Arm's collaboration with Nvidia on the Grace Blackwell GB200 chip, which combines Nvidia's GPU with an Arm-based CPU, as an accelerant for Arm's data center growth.
The article notes that Arm has designs with Microsoft's Cobalt, indicating a positive relationship between the two companies.
